How I Work

My design process.

Case Study 01

Learning Management System

Designed an LMS platform to simplify online learning for students, mentors, and administrators — a learner-facing website plus a full admin dashboard, designed solo across a 3-month internship.

Role: UI/UX Designer Duration: 3 months Tool: Figma
  • 40+ Screens
  • 3 User Roles
  • 15+ Components
  • 1 Design System
  • Fully Responsive Layout
00 · OVERVIEW

Two products, one connected ecosystem.

The LMS combines a learner-focused website with a feature-rich admin dashboard to streamline the entire learning ecosystem — from registration and course enrollment to curriculum management, assessments and progress tracking. I owned the UX research, user flows, wireframes, IA and high-fidelity prototypes for both sides, working closely with the dev team to keep the experience consistent end to end.

01 · THE PROBLEM

Learning was scattered across disconnected tools.

Students struggled to discover relevant courses and track their progress through confusing navigation. Admins were stuck managing courses, users and curriculum through manual, disconnected processes with no central view.

01Complex course management workflows
02Difficult, unstructured curriculum creation
03Manual user & mentor management
04Poor course discovery experience
05Limited visibility into learner progress
06Inconsistent navigation across modules
02 · RESEARCH & PERSONAS

Studying five LMS platforms, then mapping two very different users.

I benchmarked Moodle, Coursera, Udemy, Skillshare and Google Classroom to understand common patterns before defining who I was actually designing for.

Primary User

Rahul Sharma, 22

College Student
Goals
  • Learn industry-relevant skills
  • Monitor learning progress
  • Learn from experienced mentors
Pain points
  • Difficult navigation
  • Hard to locate purchased courses
Secondary User

Priya Nair, 30

LMS Administrator
Goals
  • Manage users efficiently
  • Create & publish courses
  • Monitor learner engagement
Pain points
  • Manual course management
  • Time-consuming curriculum creation
03 · STUDENT FLOW

From landing page to certificate in nine steps.

Landing Page→ Google Login→ Explore Courses→ Enroll→ My Courses→ Assessment→ Certificate
Login & Sign Up
LoginSecure sign-in screen for returning students.
Sign upOnboarding form to create a new student account.
App Screens
Onboarding offerPersonalized offer shown right after sign-up to boost activation.
Personalized learner dashboardAt-a-glance view of ongoing courses, performance and quick actions.
Course discovery & enrollmentBrowse and compare available courses with pricing and mentor info.
My Courses libraryPurchased course library showing progress on each enrolled course.
Course detail with curriculumFull curriculum breakdown before a student commits to enroll.
Course lesson playerVideo lesson view with a session-by-session curriculum sidebar.
Task instructionsRules and scoring criteria shown before a timed task begins.
Course quizTimed multiple-choice quiz with live progress and countdown.
Task trackingCentral view of task completion across every enrolled course.
Course completionCelebratory summary screen shown after finishing a course.
Meet your mentorsDirectory of mentors students can learn from and follow.
My favoritesSaved courses students want to revisit or purchase later.
Profile settingsEditable personal details and account information.
Cart & order summaryOrder summary before checkout, with pricing breakdown.
Rate your learning experiencePost-course survey rating content quality and pacing.
Rate your mentorFeedback form rating the mentor's teaching and support.
PaymentCard payment screen with saved cards and multiple payment options.
Admin Panel Login
Admin secure loginRestricted sign-in gate for platform administrators.
Admin Management
Admin roles & permissionsOverview of all admins with role and status controls.
Add adminQuick modal to invite a new admin and assign a role.
Edit adminUpdate an existing admin's details and permissions.
Delete confirmationSafeguard step before permanently removing an admin.
Suspend confirmationWarns of access loss before suspending an admin account.
Log out confirmationSimple confirmation to prevent accidental sign-outs.
User Management
User management tableSearchable directory of all students with status & activity.
Student profile & enrollmentsDetailed learner profile with enrolled course progress.
Course Management
Course management libraryLibrary of all published courses with quick stats.
Drag-and-drop curriculum builderStep-by-step tool for structuring sections, units & tasks.
Review and publish courseFinal review of course details before going live.
Task question editorEditor for building MCQ, True/False & multi-choice tasks.
Course & ratings analyticsEnrollment trends and learner feedback ratings at a glance.
Learner progress trackingPer-student completion status across an entire course.
Mentor Management
Mentor management gridDirectory of all mentors available to assign to courses.
Mentor profile detailFull mentor profile with rating and course history.
Mentor onboarding formForm to onboard a new mentor and set specialization.
Feedback
Mentor feedback overviewAggregated ratings on mentor teaching quality.
Feedback respondents listIndividual student responses behind a feedback score.
04 · DESIGN SYSTEM

One consistent language across 40+ screens.

A single component library — buttons, cards, tables, forms, status chips, progress bars — kept the learner site and admin dashboard feeling like one product rather than two.

Primary CTA
Navy sidebar
Background
Cards & sections
Success
Warning
Typography — Poppins
H1 · 40px Bold· H2 · 32px Semi Bold· H3 · 24px Semi Bold· Body · 16px Regular· Caption · 12px Medium
Reusable components
ButtonsCourse CardsMentor Cards Stat CardsProgress BarsTables ModalsStatus ChipsPaginationDropdowns
05 · PROTOTYPE & INTERACTIONS

Every state, wired up before a single line of code.

Interactive Figma prototypes simulated real interactions — Google login, cart-to-checkout, curriculum building, profile editing — so developers knew exactly how each screen should behave.

→Button hover & active states
→Card hover effects
→Dropdown animations
→Progress bar updates
→Form validation feedback
→Smooth page transitions
06 · RESPONSIVE DESIGN

One system, three screen sizes.

Every flow was designed to adapt cleanly from a full multi-column admin dashboard down to a touch-friendly mobile experience.

Desktop

Full dashboard layout

  • Sidebar navigation
  • Multi-column cards
  • Advanced data tables
Tablet

Collapsible layout

  • Collapsible sidebar
  • Responsive cards
  • Simplified navigation
▤Mobile login & course browsing
▤Simplified mobile dashboard
▤Touch-friendly buttons & menus
▤Responsive course details
07 · ACCESSIBILITY

Designed to be usable by everyone.

01Sufficient color contrast for readability
02Readable type sizes with consistent hierarchy
03Logical keyboard navigation
04Visible focus states on interactive elements
05Touch targets sized for mobile usability
06Consistent, predictable navigation patterns
08 · KEY DECISIONS

Why the product looks the way it does.

Decision

Orange as the primary color

Conveys energy & motivation, drawing attention to key actions like "Enroll" and "Publish."
Decision

Fixed sidebar for admin

Quick access to management tools without overwhelming the interface.
Decision

Google Sign-In

Simplifies onboarding and reduces friction for first-time learners.
Decision

Step-by-step curriculum builder

Turns a complex authoring task into a guided, manageable workflow.
09 · CHALLENGES & SOLUTIONS

What was hard, and how it got solved.

↳Two very different experiences, one consistent system — solved with a shared component library
↳Large volumes of course & user data — organized with cards, tables & clear hierarchy
↳A powerful but simple curriculum builder — solved with a step-by-step workflow
↳Complex assessment creation (MCQ, True/False, Multiple Choice) — streamlined into one task editor
↳Consistency across 40+ screens — enforced through reusable components
↳Informative dashboards that don't overwhelm — solved with focused cards & filters
10 · OUTCOME

What shipped.

  • Designed 40+ high-fidelity responsive screens
  • Built complete student & admin workflows
  • Improved course discovery through intuitive navigation
  • Simplified enrollment & learning workflows
  • Reduced admin complexity via a centralized dashboard
  • Built a scalable curriculum management system
  • Established a design system for future scalability
  • Delivered developer-ready UI in Figma
11 · WHAT I LEARNED

How this internship shaped me as a designer.

  • Designing scalable design systems with reusable components
  • Balancing the different needs of learners & administrators
  • Structuring complex information through IA
  • Creating intuitive flows for multi-role applications
  • Collaborating with developers on implementation-ready designs
  • Strengthening research, wireframing, prototyping & responsive UI skills
12 · PROCESS TIMELINE

From discovery to developer handoff.

Discover→ Research→ Personas→ IA→ User Flows→ Wireframes→ High-Fidelity UI→ Prototype→ Developer Handoff
